info.name: RBAC – Roles (/admin/rbac/roles)
info.description:
E2E tests for the roles CRUD API and role-permission grant management.

Routes covered:
  GET    /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles
  POST   /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles
  GET    /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles/{id}
  PATCH  /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles/{id}
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles/{id}
  GET    /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles/{id}/permissions
  POST   /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles/{id}/permissions
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/rbac/roles/{id}/permissions/{perm_id}

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-rbac):
  1. setup            — register + Gmail OTP + verify-email + login
                        (captures roles_access_token — plain user, no RBAC permissions yet)
  2. auth-guard       — 401 on GET /roles and POST /roles with no Authorization header
  3. rbac-guard       — 403 on rbac:read and rbac:manage routes with valid JWT but no permissions
  4. owner-bootstrap  — POST /owner/bootstrap promotes the test user to owner
                        (owner role grants rbac:read + rbac:manage; RBAC middleware reads DB
                        per request — no re-login needed; the same token becomes effective)
  5. happy-path       — full CRUD flow: list → create → get → update → list-perms →
                        add-perm → list-perms-after → remove-perm → delete role
  6. not-found        — 404 cases for all routes that accept an {id} URL param
  7. validation       — 422/400/413 input-error cases for POST and PATCH routes
  8. immutable-guard  — 409 when attempting to update or delete a system role

Guard order (handler):
  GET    routes: JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:read)   → handler
  mutate routes: JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:manage) → handler

The admin router applies AllowContentType("application/json") before JWTAuth, so POST/PATCH
requests in auth-guard must carry Content-Type: application/json to reach the 401 guard.

Environment variables required:
  bootstrap_secret   — must match BOOTSTRAP_SECRET in the running server's .env
  gmail_test_email   — Gmail address for OTP receipt
  gmail_access_token — OAuth2 token for Gmail API reads

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by unit tests):
  - DB / store errors → 500                    — handler_test.go, service_test.go
  - UUID parse failure → ErrRoleNotFound       — service_test.go (invalid UUID string)
  - Conditions JSON default '{}' applied        — service_test.go
  - nil roles marshals as [] not null           — handler_test.go

Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before each suite run.
Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so admin-route requests never collide on any shared bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the OTP via Gmail API, verifies email, and logs in. Captures roles_access_token (plain user — no RBAC permissions yet). This same token is reused for rbac-guard (→ 403) and, after owner-bootstrap promotes the user, for all subsequent folders (→ 200/201/204). The RBAC middleware reads permissions from DB on each request so no re-login is needed after bootstrap.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API (async)...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "roles_code":', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] collection variable "roles_code" is empty — Gmail fetch did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set and not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Captured roles_access_token (plain user — no RBAC permissions yet).');

item.name: auth-guard
item.description: Verifies that the JWTAuth middleware rejects requests with no Authorization header before RBAC or handler logic is reached. Tests a representative read route (GET /roles) and a representative mutate route (POST /roles). POST carries Content-Type: application/json so the AllowContentType router middleware passes before JWTAuth fires.

exec: // JWTAuth fires before RBAC.Require — no token means 401 before any handler logic.
// JWTAuth fires next — no token → 401 before RBAC or body decode.

item.name: rbac-guard
item.description: Verifies that RBAC.Require rejects a valid JWT belonging to a plain user with no rbac:read or rbac:manage permission. The JWT is valid (from setup/login) but the user has not yet been bootstrapped as owner. Must return 403 before the handler is called. owner-bootstrap runs after this folder.

exec: // JWTAuth passes. RBAC.Require(rbac:read) fires next — user has no owner role → 403.
// JWTAuth passes. RBAC.Require(rbac:manage) fires next → 403 before body decode.

item.name: owner-bootstrap
item.description: Promotes the setup user to owner by calling POST /owner/bootstrap with the correct bootstrap_secret. The owner role carries all permissions including rbac:read and rbac:manage. RBAC.Require reads the DB on every request — no re-login is needed; the existing roles_access_token is sufficient for all subsequent requests.

item.name: POST /owner/bootstrap → 201 (grant owner role to roles user)

exec: console.error('[ERROR] roles_access_token not set — setup/POST /login did not complete.');
console.error('[ERROR] bootstrap_secret not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
console.log('[INFO] roles user is now owner — RBAC.Require(rbac:read) and RBAC.Require(rbac:manage) will pass on subsequent requests.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Full CRUD flow authenticated as owner (rbac:read + rbac:manage granted by owner-bootstrap).

Execution order:
  1. GET /admin/permissions            — capture roles_perm_id for the AddRolePermission step
  2. GET /rbac/roles                   — assert non-empty list; capture roles_system_role_id
  3. POST /rbac/roles                  — create test role; capture roles_role_id
  4. GET /rbac/roles/{id}              — assert created role shape
  5. PATCH /rbac/roles/{id}            — update name + description
  6. GET /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions  — assert empty list before adding
  7. POST /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions — add a permission grant → 204
  8. GET /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions  — assert list now contains one entry; capture roles_grant_perm_id
  9. DELETE /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions/{perm_id} — remove the grant → 204
  10. DELETE /rbac/roles/{id}          — delete the test role → 204

exec: // Capture the first permission ID for use in the AddRolePermission step.

item.name: GET /rbac/roles → 200 (list includes seeded roles; capture system role id)

exec: pm.test('roles list is non-empty (seed data present)', () => {
pm.test('each role has id, name, is_system_role, is_owner_role, is_active, created_at', () => {
pm.test('owner role is present in the list', () => {
pm.expect(found, 'no role with is_owner_role=true in list').to.be.true;
// Capture the first system role ID for the immutable-guard folder.
console.warn('[WARN] No system role found in list — immutable-guard tests will be skipped.');
if (!id) { console.error('[ERROR] roles_role_id not set — POST /rbac/roles did not complete.'); }
pm.test('description is updated', () => { pm.expect(body.description).to.eql('Updated by E2E test suite'); });
pm.test('permissions list is empty (no grants yet)', () => {
console.error('[ERROR] roles_perm_id not set — GET /admin/permissions step did not complete.');
// 204 No Content — no body, no Set-Cookie.

item.name: GET /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions → 200 (one entry after grant; capture roles_grant_perm_id)

exec: pm.test('permissions list has exactly one entry', () => {
// Capture permission_id for the subsequent RemoveRolePermission request.
console.error('[ERROR] roles_grant_perm_id not set — GET /permissions after grant did not complete.');

item.name: not-found
item.description: Verifies that routes accepting an {id} URL parameter return 404 with the correct code when the ID is a valid UUID that does not correspond to any row. Uses static UUIDs guaranteed not to exist after a clean DB run.

item.name: POST /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions — valid role, unknown permission → 404 permission_not_found

exec: // Create a temporary role so the service can proceed past the role-exists check
// and reach the permission-exists check where it returns ErrPermissionNotFound.
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ name: 'e2e-perm-not-found-' + Date.now() }) }
console.error('[ERROR] Could not create temp role:', err || res.code);
}, (err) => { if (err) console.warn('[WARN] Temp role cleanup failed:', err); });

item.name: validation
item.description:
Body decode and field validation cases for POST /rbac/roles, PATCH /rbac/roles/{id}, and POST /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions. All requests carry a valid JWT. Validation fires after RBAC.Require but before any service or DB call — no state is mutated.

Rules from validators.go:
  createRole:        name required and trimmed; name <= 100 chars
  updateRole:        at least one field (name or description) must be present; if name present, must be non-empty and <= 100 chars
  addRolePermission: permission_id required; granted_reason required; access_type in {direct,conditional,request,denied}; scope in {own,all}; permission_id must be a valid UUID (handler rejects before service)

exec: // validateCreateRole trims name before checking emptiness.
pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ name: 'any', _pad: pad }) });

item.name: PATCH /rbac/roles/{id} — no fields → 422 (at least one required)

exec: // validateUpdateRole: both Name and Description nil → ErrNoUpdateFields.
// Validation fires before any DB call so the unknown UUID does not trigger 404.
// Name is explicitly set to empty string — fails ErrNameEmpty.
// Validation fires before service so the unknown UUID does not trigger 404.
// access_type must be one of: direct, conditional, request, denied.
// scope must be one of: own, all.
// uuid.Parse(req.PermissionID) fails — handler returns 422 before calling service.

item.name: immutable-guard
item.description: Verifies that rbac.ErrSystemRoleImmutable maps to 409 system_role_immutable when attempting to update or delete a system role. Uses roles_system_role_id captured during happy-path/GET /rbac/roles. Both requests are safe to repeat — the role is never mutated.

exec: console.warn('[WARN] roles_system_role_id not set — check happy-path/GET /rbac/roles captured a system role.');
if (!id) { console.warn('[WARN] roles_system_role_id not set — test skipped.'); return; }
// Service returns rbac.ErrSystemRoleImmutable → handler maps to 409.
console.warn('[WARN] roles_system_role_id not set — skipping immutable DELETE test.');
if (!id) { console.warn('[WARN] roles_system_role_id not set — test skipped.'); return; }

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email. Used by setup/register, setup/verify-email, and setup/login.
Password for the roles test user. Set in setup/register prerequest.
6-digit OTP fetched asynchronously from Gmail during setup/register test script. Consumed by setup/verify-email.

JWT access_token from setup/login. Plain user before owner-bootstrap; owner after. The RBAC middleware reads permissions from DB on each request so this same token works for both rbac-guard (403) and happy-path (200/201/204) without re-issuing.
Permission ID captured from GET /admin/permissions[0].id. Used as the permission_id argument to POST /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions in the happy-path and not-found folders.
ID of the first system role found in GET /rbac/roles. Used by the immutable-guard folder to assert 409 on PATCH and DELETE. Left empty if no system role is seeded.
ID of the test role created by POST /rbac/roles in the happy-path folder. Used for all subsequent happy-path operations and deleted at the end of the happy-path folder.
permission_id from the first entry in GET /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions after AddRolePermission. Used as the {perm_id} URL parameter for DELETE /rbac/roles/{id}/permissions/{perm_id}.
Temporary role ID created inline during the not-found/permission_not_found test. Cleaned up in that test's post-script.
